experimental_model: SNCA triplication/A53T iPSC dopaminergic neurons or alpha-synuclein PFF model
primary_assays: pS129 alpha-synuclein, aggregate load, synaptic/neurite phenotypes
secondary_endpoints: pS129 alpha-synuclein, aggregate seeding, synaptic marker preservation, neurite length
positive_controls: alpha-synuclein PFF seeding for phenotype induction; known aggregation-modifying control where available
negative_controls: vehicle control; isogenic corrected or wild-type control where applicable; non-targeting guide/siRNA for perturbation assays
toxicity_counterscreen: cell viability, neurite integrity, apoptosis/necrosis markers, mitochondrial stress and off-target inflammatory activation
go_criteria: >=25% improvement in primary disease-relevant endpoint with preserved viability and reproduced in independent line/batch
no_go_criteria: no reproducible target engagement, worsening viability/inflammation, or discordant results across independent clones/batches
replication_plan: minimum two independent iPSC lines or donors per genotype where feasible, blinded image analysis, technical triplicates and independent replication
recommended_next_experiment: Use alpha-synuclein PFF or SNCA-mutant iPSC dopaminergic neurons with pS129 aggregation, synaptic, and viability endpoints.

toxicity_counterscreen: cell viability, neurite integrity, apoptosis/necrosis markers, mitochondrial stress and off-target inflammatory activation
go_criteria: statistically robust target engagement plus directionally favourable survival, mitochondrial or inflammatory endpoint
no_go_criteria: no reproducible target engagement, worsening viability/inflammation, or discordant results across independent clones/batches
replication_plan: minimum two independent iPSC lines or donors per genotype where feasible, blinded image analysis, technical triplicates and independent replication
recommended_next_experiment: Test GLP-1 agonists in metabolic/inflammatory stress co-culture with neuronal survival, cytokines, and mitochondrial respiration endpoints.
